name: Xolotl
description: Xolotl, the Aztec god of lightning, fire, death, monsters, misfortune, sickness, and deformities, is often depicted as a dog-headed man or a skeleton. He is the twin brother of Quetzalcoatl, and together they represent duality and transformation. Xolotl played a crucial role in the creation myths, specifically in the Fifth Sun, where he helped Quetzalcoatl retrieve the bones of previous generations from the underworld, Mictlan, to recreate humanity. He is known for his self-sacrificing nature, as he initially resisted being sacrificed to set the sun in motion, transforming himself into various forms like an agave plant, a double corn stalk, and an axolotl to avoid his fate. This association with transformation and duality makes him a complex and intriguing figure in Aztec mythology. He is also considered the psychopomp, guiding the dead on their journey to Mictlan and protecting the sun as it travels through the underworld each night. Xolotl's canine form reflects his connection to the underworld and his role as a guardian, while his association with monsters and deformities highlights the darker aspects of the cosmos and the unpredictable nature of life. His name translates to 'animal', and he embodies the wild, untamed forces of nature. He is a god of transitions, both physical and spiritual, and a reminder of the cyclical nature of existence.
subtitle: God of lightning, dogs, and the underworld.

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Humanity
keyMyths.description: Xolotl helps Quetzalcoatl retrieve the bones of previous humans from Mictlan to create the current generation, demonstrating his vital role in the Aztec creation myth.

keyMyths.title: Xolotl's Sacrifice
keyMyths.description: To set the Fifth Sun in motion, the gods had to sacrifice themselves. Xolotl resisted, transforming into various forms before ultimately succumbing, showing his connection to transformation.
keyMyths.source: Historia de los Mexicanos por sus pinturas

keyMyths.title: Guiding the Dead
keyMyths.description: As a psychopomp, Xolotl guides the souls of the deceased through the perils of Mictlan, the underworld, ensuring their safe passage to the afterlife.
